isolation.role: build group — runs npx opennextjs-cloudflare build

steps.expected: File exists with name, compatibility_date >= 2024-09-23, nodejs_compat flag, assets config
steps.observed: name: personal-website, compatibility_date: 2025-04-01, flags: [nodejs_compat], assets: { directory: .open-next/assets, binding: ASSETS }

steps.expected: build remains 'next build', preview and deploy use opennextjs-cloudflare CLI
steps.observed: build: 'next build', preview: 'opennextjs-cloudflare build && opennextjs-cloudflare preview', deploy: 'opennextjs-cloudflare build && opennextjs-cloudflare deploy'
.open-next listed under '# Deployment' section, no .vercel entry present
assets/ directory exists with _next/, icons/, images/, BUILD_ID, and placeholder files
Exit code 0, up to date, 803 packages audited
assets/ directory contains _next/ (JS chunks), icons/, images/, and static files

assertions.title: Blog SSG works with OpenNext adapter

steps.action: Check build log for blog page generation
steps.expected: Blog pages listed as SSG (●) with generateStaticParams

steps.action: Verify cache files for all blog slugs
steps.expected: Cache files exist for each slug

steps.observed: ai-secretary.cache confirmed to contain HTML markup

steps.action: Verify generateStaticParams ran at build time
steps.observed: ● symbol in route table confirms SSG at build time in Node.js, not on Worker

frictions.description: OpenNext shows warnings about Windows compatibility: 'OpenNext is not fully compatible with Windows. For optimal performance, it is recommended to use Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL).' Build still succeeds.
frictions.resolution: Warnings are informational only - build completes successfully on Windows

summary: Tested 7 assertions: all 7 passed. Build configuration is correct (OpenNext adapter, wrangler.jsonc, scripts, .gitignore), full build succeeds with exit code 0 producing worker.js and assets, and all 3 blog posts are pre-rendered via SSG at build time.
